parameters.name: method
parameters.label: Method
parameters.description: There are three different reordering methods. You can enter a Numeric Pattern, a Character Pattern, or use an optional second input as an order reference.

parameters.name: orderref
parameters.label: Order Reference
parameters.description: Only enabled if a second input is present. Specifies the format of that input.

parameters.name: numpattern
parameters.label: Numeric Pattern
parameters.description: This reorders the channels by channel number. Normally the index order is 0,1,2,3... etc.. The first channel is at index 0. Standard numeric patterns are allowed such as or .

parameters.name: charpattern
parameters.label: Character Pattern
parameters.description: This reorders the channels by channel name. Standard character patterns are allowed such as or or . See and Channel Name Matching Options p. 102 in the section, Standard Options of CHOPs.

parameters.name: seed
parameters.label: Seed
parameters.description: Only available if is set to "Random", specify any number, integer or non-integer, which starts the random number generator. Each number gives completely different patterns, but with similar characteri

parameters.name: nvalue
parameters.label: N Value
parameters.description: Only available if is set to "Merge N Groups" or "Every Nth ".

parameters.name: rempos
parameters.label: Remaining Position
parameters.description: Channels that do not match are called "remaining" and can also be ordered: they can be placed at the At Beginning or At Ending (in reference to the position of the matched channels).

parameters.name: remorder
parameters.label: Remaining Order
parameters.description: The channels that did not match can have the Same as Input order, or can be sorted AlphaNumerically.

parameters.name: timeslice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: Turning this on forces the channels to be " ".  A is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a string on the Common page. See .

parameters.name: srselect
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description: Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Op

parameters.name: exportmethod
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description: This will determine how to connect the channel to the parameter. Refer to the article for more information.

parameters.name: autoexportroot
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by are relative to.

parameters.name: exporttable
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: The used to hold the export information when using the Table Methods (See above).

examples.description: Common settings from 7 real Derivative examples
